reasoning: 2024 is almost certainly going to be the hottest year on record, driven by the lingering effects of the strong 2023-2024 El Niño. 2025 is expected to see cooler conditions due to an emerging La Niña or ENSO-neutral state. Consequently, climate models and leading meteorological organizations (such as the UK Met Office) forecast that 2025 will be slightly cooler than 2024. Given the typical cooling effect of La Niña, the probability of 2025 breaking the temperature record set in 2024 (or 2023) is extremely low. The market freeze value of roughly 2% perfectly reflects this low probability.
